background: A local council was considering introducing extensive recycling facilities. As part of a survey investigating the attitudes of the public towards the popularity of this initiative, the effect of age of the respondent was taken into account. Respondents were asked to score their enthusiasm towards recycling on a scale of 1 to 5 (where 5 was very enthusiastic) and their age was classed as: up to 25; from 26 to 50; or over 51 years. The following data were collected in a table format.

task.query: Carry out Kruskal-Wallis one-way analysis of variance using ranks on the data. Provide the rank means for each age group, degrees of freedom (df), H statistic, and P-value.
task.answer: Rank means: Up to 25 = 18.85, 26 to 50 = 8.15, Over 51 = 19.5. Degrees of freedom (df): 2. H statistic: 10.483 (corrected: 10.984). P-value: <0.01 (exact, corrected: 0.0041).

task.query: Using multiple comparison tests where appropriate, what do you conclude from this analysis?
task.answer: There is a significant difference among age groups as to enthusiasm towards recycling. Nemenyi's multiple comparisons reveal that those aged up to 25 and over 51 do not differ in their enthusiasm, but both age groups are significantly more enthusiastic than those aged 26-50 (P<0.05): (up to 25, over 51) > 26 to 50.

meta_info.chapter: Differences between more than two samples
meta_info.book: Using Statistics to Understand the Environment
